characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: '80s Hair: Flashbacks to the 80s in the 2nd movie reveal that Carrie wore her hair in the busy perm upon her arrival in Manhattan and then wore her hair a la Madonna in her "Material Girl" phase after settling in the city. Aesop Amnesia: InAnd Just Like Thatshe decides to try things again with Aidan after he got amicably divorced from his wife, but their previous issues of Aidan being unable to forgive Carrie for cheating mean it goes disastrously wrong. All Girls Want Bad Boys: Tends to date punkers and greasers. Kurt and Twentysomething Sam are examples.Also cheating on dependable Aiden with the elusive Mr. Big. All Women Love Shoes: She spends aridiculousamount of money on shoes, and wears said expensive shoes pretty much everywhere.Carrie:I've spent $40,000 on shoes and I have no place to live? I will literally be the old woman who lived in her shoes! Ambiguously Jewish: She doesn't seem to celebrate/be familiar with Christian holidays and is played by a Jewish actress.

characters.name: Carrie Bradshaw
characters.description: '80s Hair: Flashbacks to the 80s in the 2nd movie reveal that Carrie wore her hair in the busy perm upon her arrival in Manhattan and then wore her hair a la Madonna in her "Material Girl" phase after settling in the city. Aesop Amnesia: InAnd Just Like Thatshe decides to try things again with Aidan after he got amicably divorced from his wife, but their previous issues of Aidan being unable to forgive Carrie for cheating mean it goes disastrously wrong. All Girls Want Bad Boys: Tends to date punkers and greasers. Kurt and Twentysomething Sam are examples.Also cheating on dependable Aiden with the elusive Mr. Big. All Women Love Shoes: She spends aridiculousamount of money on shoes, and wears said expensive shoes pretty much everywhere.Carrie:I've spent $40,000 on shoes and I have no place to live? I will literally be the old woman who lived in her shoes! Ambiguously Jewish: She doesn't seem to celebrate/be familiar with Christian holidays and is played by a Jewish actress.

characters.name: Samantha Jones
characters.description: '80s Hair: In a brief flashback to the 80s in the 2nd movie, she wears her hair so crimped, teased, and long, and bouffant it would put a Heavy Metal rocker orPeg Bundyto shame. A-Cup Angst: Not as an extreme example as most, but in the later seasons when she starts dating Smith, she decides to get a "tasteful" boob job...then she finds out she has breast cancer. Affectionate Nickname: Calls her friends "honey". All Women Are Lustful: And proud of it! Berserk Button:She doesnotlike beingSlut Shamed.Don't tell Samantha who she can and can't date.

characters.name: Charlotte York
characters.description: '80s Hair: A brief flashback in the 2nd movie shows that she has always worn her hair long, shiny, and preppy albeit with heavy bangs of the decade. Beware the Nice Ones: Charlotte is usually the sweetest and most forgiving of the four, but she isn't afraid to speak her mind if she thinks something is wrong, likecalling out Carrie for cheating on Aidan with Big, or slapping her friend's husband when he admits he wants to leave her for Charlotte. Brainy Brunette: She can be naive and a bit ditzy but she is also sophisticated and well educated- thanks to her blue- blooded upbringing. It was mentioned in one episode that she was a Straight A Student. Break the Cutie: Charlotte may come from fine stock, but growing up with such high expectations for yourself makes it that much more difficult when they can't be met. First she discovers, on the eve of her (very expensive) wedding, that her fiancécan't get it up; then, once that problem is solved, she has difficulty conceiving, and when she floats the idea of adoption her mother-in-law makes it known that she won't tolerate a "Mandarin baby"; she divorces Trey, and yay! Falls in love again! But it turns out he's super Jewish, so in order to be with the man she loves she needs to convert and (at least in her mind) abandon Christmas for Hanukkah; then Harry leaves her indefinitely after she spent all day cooking an enormous dinner for him but then says something she shouldn't have; they get back together, but then later she has a miscarriage, then the couple that had agreed to have their baby adopted changes their mind.Everything turns out all right in the end, but dammit if Charlotte didn't get put through the wringer in order to get there. Cute, but Cacophonic: She's mostly a very sweet and friendly optimist, but she canshriek. In the first movie when Carrie announces her engagement to Big, she screams so loudly everyone in the restaurant stares at them and Miranda complains her ears are still ringing.

characters.name: Miranda Hobbes
characters.description: '80s Hair: In a brief 80s flashback in the 2nd movie, she is seen with a bouffant, Pat Benatar bowl cut. Accidental Adultery: In season 2 ofAnd Just Like That,Miranda is completely unaware that her love interest Che Diaz is still legally married to their husband Kyle and is shocked when she learns the truth. Ambiguously Bi: It's unclear if this was unintentional or not (especially complicated by Cynthia Nixon who was at the time of shooting most episodes in the closet), but Miranda absolutely gives off this impression. From her constant jokes and asides about "sexually offering herself up to her friends", to her fashion sense (which ranges from conventionally feminine to fabulously butch) and general issues with intimacy (even while dating Steve) there's this sense of sexual frustration that permeates her character throughout the show's runtime. She explores the idea of potentially being into women early on, but brushes it off after a kiss with a lesbian woman.Subverted in the sequel seriesAnd Just Like That, whereshe discovers she is bisexual/pansexual. Subverted in the sequel seriesAnd Just Like That, whereshe discovers she is bisexual/pansexual. Anger Born of Worry: Miranda yells at Steve when he confides to finding a lump in one of his testicles, telling him that it IS a big deal and he needs to get it tested as soon as possible. She feels bad about it later, but Steve actually thanks her for being concerned enough to call him an idiot for trying to ignore the issue.

characters.name: Seema Patel
characters.description: Riches to Rags: In season 3 she is pretty much fired from her successful realty firm, and has to start over on her own, causing her to have to make some changes in her lifestyle. Sixth Ranger: She’s a much later addition to the friend group and the main cast. Uptown Girl: She starts dating down to Earth working classGranola GuyAdam in the 3rd season.

characters.name: Lisa Todd Wexley
characters.description: Sixth Ranger: Much like Seema, she joins the group after meeting Charlotte via the school their children attend in the sequel series.

characters.name: Big
characters.description: No Name Given: To the audience, anyway, symbolizing Carrie's struggle to get close to him emotionally. It's finally revealed in the series finale and subsequentmovie:John James Preston. Not So Stoic: Is heartbroken by an unemotionally uninvolved movie star and confides in this to Carrie. Later in the series he is moved by Carrie's book and very apologetic about hurting her in the past.

characters.name: Aidan Shaw
characters.description: Forgiven, but Not Forgotten: Never manages to forget Carrie's betrayal nor truly trust her. Grand Romantic Gesture: Attempts these with Carrie when they get back together, but it's aDeconstructed Tropeas the gestures are to cover up a glaring flaw in their relationship - he doesn't trust her after what she did. Nice Guy: Positioned as this in contrast to the emotionally unavailable Mr Big and he certainly takes alotof crap from Carrie over the course of their relationship. The Bus Came Back: After their break-up, Carrie runs into him briefly in 'To Market, to Market', where he is shown to beHappily Marriedand father to a baby son.He appears again in the second movie, meeting Carrie in Dubai.They kiss, though Carrie confesses to Big and returns to him.And again inAnd Just Like That....This time he and Carrie rekindle their romance, though it's complicated by Carrie's relationship with Aidan's children and his aversion to staying in her apartment (a place he still associates with the breakdown of their past relationship). He appears again in the second movie, meeting Carrie in Dubai.They kiss, though Carrie confesses to Big and returns to him.

characters.name: Aleksandr Petrovsky
characters.description: Amicable Exes: Aleksandr is still on good terms with his first wife, Juliet, and both speak highly of each other. However when Carrie speaks with her, Juliet reminisces about the happy times but notes that she was always second to his work mirroring the situation that Carrie finds herself in. Grand Romantic Gesture: His entire relationship to Carrie is made up of these. May–December Romance: With Carrie, which causes problems later in the relationship. No Guy Wants to Be Chased: Aleksandr initially becomes interested in Carrie because she doesn't fawn all over him or his work, unlike Charlotte who was fangirling to talk to him. He tries several times to ask Carrie out over the phone and doesn't mind that she doesn't remember who he is right away. Real Men Cook: Makes Carrie dinner in her own house.

characters.name: TreyMacDougal
characters.description: Amicable Exes: When his mother tries to take the apartment he gave Charlotte away because she didn't honor her marriage, Trey comes to her defense by sending a telegram saying she was a wonderful wife and tells his mother to leave her alone. Birds of a Feather: For Charlotte. Unfortunately, his intimacy problems in marriage drive a wedge through what brought them together. Caught with Your Pants Down: Charlotte catches him after he tells her he's not a sexual person. I Call Him "Mr. Happy":Charlotte:You pull me off my unicorn, you tear away my gossamer petticoats, and you put your 'Schooner' deep inside my 'Rebecca'. Jizzed in My Pants: When Trey can get sexually aroused, it usually leads to this.

characters.name: Che Diaz
characters.description: Accidental Adultery:In season 1, Che was unaware that Miranda was still married to Steve and not in an open marriage like they first assumed. When Che finds out the truth, they are devastated by the idea of being a homewrecker and dumps Miranda until her divorce is finalized. In season 2, Che forgot to tell Miranda that they themselves are still legally married to their ex-husband Kyle and only reveals the fact after inviting their ex-husband Kyle for a threesome with Miranda. Betty and Veronica: Che is the Veronica toSteve's Betty over Miranda's Archie. Black Jezebel Stereotype: Much of Che's characterization involves them having lots of sex withMiranda Hobbes, engaging in threesomes with their not-quite ex-husband and smoking weed. Closet Key:They're this to Miranda Hobbes who didn't realize she was bisexual/pansexual prior to meeting them. Everybody Has Standards: Che loves dating guys and girls,but isn't willing to jeopardize marriages or be a homewrecker to someone's family. So when they discover that Miranda is not in an open marriage, they break it off.

characters.name: Harry Goldenblatt
characters.description: Brutal Honesty:Verycomfortable speaking his mind. Foil: To TreyMacDougal. Trey is tall, dark and handsome. Harry is short, bald and pudgy. However, Trey has massive intimacy issues that not only make sex with Charlotte extremely difficult, but he is emotionally crippled due to his relationship with his mother. Harry on the other hand isfarmore emotionally stable, and hasnoproblem not only speaking his mind, but also pleasing her sexually. Happily Married: With Charlotte. The two absolutely adore one another and are still very much in love and happy two decades on. Irony: Meets his future wife Charlotte because he's the divorce lawyer she chooses to handle her case.In addition, Harry is the exact opposite of the type of man Charlotte fantasized about, but he proves to be her best romantic partner. In addition, Harry is the exact opposite of the type of man Charlotte fantasized about, but he proves to be her best romantic partner.

characters.name: Lily York Goldenblatt
characters.description: Generation Xerox: She is a lot like her mother. Happily Adopted: She seems to have no angst about her adoption and loved her parents.

characters.name: Rock Goldenblatt
characters.description: Sibling Yin-Yang: Lily takes after Charlotte while Rock is a lot more of a tomboy.

characters.name: Steve Brady
characters.description: Amicable Exes: He and Miranda have an on-off relationship throughout most of the series, but they are almost always at least on good terms. Deadpan Snarker: Often. A great example is when he and Miranda are shopping for fittings for their wedding and his shoe gets caught on a particularly strong piece of chewed gum.Miranda:This issous! We should just get married right here on this street.Steve:We might have to if I can't get this gum off my shoe!Which leads to a"Eureka!" Momentwhen they notice a particularly nice park nearby.Miranda:...I don't hate it!Steve:Me too! Which leads to a"Eureka!" Momentwhen they notice a particularly nice park nearby. Dogged Nice Guy: Towards Miranda. She really puts him through the wringer before they start a family and finally get married. Law of Inverse Fertility: Accidentally gets Miranda pregnant, despite him having only one ball and her having a "lazy ovary."

characters.name: Brady Hobbes
characters.description: Nom de Mom: He has his mom’s maiden name as his last name. Though he has his

characters.name: Stanford Blatch
characters.description: Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Anthony, for a while. The Lancer: He is sometimes Carrie's. Pair the Spares: Averted, both of them are offended when Charlotte assumes they will like each other just because they are gay. It takes them a while to get to know each other, first. Pet Homosexual: He started out as Carrie's, but he does get his own storylines and calls Carrie out when she acts like his love life with Marcus is of nominal importance. Platonic Life-Partners: With Carrie.

characters.name: Anthony Marentino
characters.description: All Gays are Promiscuous: Averted — In the movie, he says he's allowed to cheat because he's Italian, not because he's gay. Stanford tells Carrie he just said it to buck tradition. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Stanford, for a while. Defrosting Ice Queen: To Stanford. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He's very snarky and obnoxious, but he really is a loyal friend to Charlotte. Large Ham: He's almost always shouting or coming out with snarky quips.
